title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: Tested Learn a song mode, riff repeater, session mode, guitarcade minigames, guitarcade score attack, custom songs, everything works. Even better than pure wine, where score attack mode had occasional crashes. Encoutered one crash, but couldn't repeat it so didn't trace it. Made these tweaks to get the Realtone cable working as they were necessary for pure wine installation as well: $(WINEPREFIX="~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa ; sed -e 's:\(Win32UltraLowLatencyMode\)=1:\1=0:g' -e 's:\(ExclusiveMode\)=1:\1=0:g' -i ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Rocksmith2014/Rocksmith.ini)
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
First it would crash with "Sorry, something went wrong"  message. I could not make it work, so I started in Windows instead, and registered in UPlay there.


To make it work work in Linux I had to do something strange, as the game seemed to look for player data in wrong place. I had to link the Steam 'userdata' directory to the system root directory (admin rights required):


ln -s $HOME/.local/share/Steam/userdata /


After this the game would start and read the account details set in Windows. Audio output would work with a bit of cracking when using laptop's speaker, but no audio input at all.


To fix the cracking I had to force 48000Hz sample rate in PulseAudio (I had it set to 44100 for some reason).


Making the input work was more complicated. It wouldn't work with the pulseaudio wine driver at all, probably because it forces 32-bit float sample rate, while Rocksmith requires 16 bit.

I could make it work with the alsa driver, provided I modified the Rocksmith.ini file, and, what seems important, I had set the default input audio device in winecfg to 'system default'. I can select output device there to whatever I like, but the input must be set to 'system default'  – I can then select specific input in the game.


My Rocksmith.ini audio settings:
[Audio]
EnableMicrophone=1
ExclusiveMode=0
LatencyBuffer=3
ForceDefaultPlaybackDevice=
ForceWDM=0
ForceDirectXSink=0
DumpAudioLog=1
MaxOutputBufferSize=0
RealToneCableOnly=0
Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0


I don't have the 'Real Tone' cable, so I have used the microphone mode instead, though not for a mic, but for a guitar connected via an USB interface. With Jack, Guitarix and all  (direct connection via ALSA or PulseAudio works too).


Minor issues:
- latency seems not perfect;
- crashes occasionally;
- the symlink in / feels quite wrong.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: The game would not start. "Something went wrong" error message.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: Realtone cable works. Very playable. Nothing bad. To start with I had trouble with "Something went wrong" error. I ran WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa. Thanks to other poster who mentioned making a symlink of the steamapps folder into the root drive. Rather than doing this, I ran WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winecfg and mapped the Z drive to my $HOME/.steam directory. It also solves the issue. With this I had it running with no sound at all. This seems to be a problem with missing 32 bit drivers. For this I ran sudo apt-get install libasound2:i386 libasound2-plugins:i386. This enabled sound. Using these settings I could get the realtone cable recognized as a mic but not as the cable. It was playable like this but my electric guitar was awful quiet as no sound came out of the speakers for the guitar. I did set ExclusiveMode=0 and Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0 in the Rocksmith.ini. But then I needed to also pavucontrol then run it. Go to the configuration tab and set the realtone cable profile  to "off". Now the realtone cable was recognized. I did not have to change any other settings including audio under winecfg. I can play songs, games, connect to UPLAY, and also import and play custom songs. It all works flawlessly although it was a real pain figuring it all out.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
I followed the steps from the success of others and managed to get it working for me as well.  Thanks to the community for sharing.
1) Set sound to use ALSA.  WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa
2) Mapped the z drive to $HOME/.steam/steam
3) Set ExclusiveMode=0 and Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0 in Rocksmith.ini
4) Set Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to Off in pavucontrol -> Configuration
5) In winecfg, set audio output devices to the outputs that my speakers are connected to (analog), left the input device as default.
6) Set Rocksmith launch options in Steam to PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%  Running without this gave me a dxvk exception when selecting a player profile.

There is still some noticable latency between plucking a string and hearing it in game (about 10ms), which is why I'm considering this to be Silver status.  To play perfectly, delay should be just barely perceptible, which it is when running natively in Windows.  Otherwise, it plays well enough that I'm no longer playing it though a VM with PCI passthrough.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: As noted in other reports you need to set alsa as output device and map steam folder to Z: to make it work. Other than that works great with noticeable input lag.
